derivation_note: Inherited verbatim from plate_calibrations/school-pa-zone (Cycle 18 agent calibration, verified). Location-level plate calibration (panels shot on this plate inherit it). Ground-plane values are art-directed estimates verified by overlay render (estimate-then-inspect), not camera-solver output. Floor-plane lines were extracted programmatically (color-boundary scan + robust least-squares) which tightens the usual eyeball error band, but the plate is hand-drawn cartoon perspective and cheats: right-wall window lines do NOT converge to the floor VP, so only floor-plane lines were used -- characters stand on the floor, so floor convergence governs scaling.

calibration.reference_object: right-wall window sill, floor-to-sill-top at the near/mid window (x=880)
calibration.note: Vertical from the baseboard/floor join (y=688, on the fitted join line y=0.584x+173) up to the sill front-top edge (y=557) at x=880; round-1 overlay showed the first estimate (122px) stopping ~9px short of the sill edge, corrected to 131. In-world sill height ~0.9 m -- chibi head height, so a chibi character standing at this depth spans roughly this many pixels. Chosen over the far-end banana-shrine pedestal (x=430, foot y=445, h=43px) because the sill is mid-ground where characters will actually be blocked, and the larger pixel span halves the relative scaling error.

ground_plane.horizon_derivation: MEASURED from three independent floor-plane lines, each extracted by color-boundary detection (tan floor vs grey locker plinth / plum baseboard / dark tile seam) and robust least-squares fit: left locker-bank base y=-1.17x+812, right baseboard-floor join y=0.584x+173, longitudinal floor-tile seam x=0.547y+141. Pairwise intersections (364,386), (346,375), (357,395) -> consensus vanishing point (355,385); adopted horizon_y=385 (+/-12 uncertainty band). VP sits left of frame center: camera looks slightly right of the hallway axis. Sanity checks: horizon is above the far-wall floor join (y~443) and below the far-wall ceiling join (y~290) as required; implied camera height ~0.38 of hallway height (low, chibi eye-level). Right-wall window top/sill lines cheat upward (hand-drawn art) and were deliberately excluded from the solve. CROSS-CHECK: the same-height window sill measured at two depths validates the horizon -- sill at x=880 is 131px with foot 688, sill at x=610 measures ~64px with foot ~529; predicted from horizon 385: 131*(529-385)/(688-385)=62px. 62~64 -> horizon confirmed within a few px.

light_sources.note: Nearest ceiling fluorescent strip fixture (two more recede down the hall at ~(410,180) and ~(390,238); this one dominates). Sampled tube color (219,234,163) -- institutional yellow-green fluorescent, top-down soft key. NON-LISTED color name: compositor will fall back to neutral grey, which is acceptable here since the cast is subtle; do not substitute 'spring green' (far too saturated).

Glowing purple PA/vending display on the far end wall above the banana shrine, sampled (174,108,211). Closest listed color is 'magenta'; actual hue is violet-purple. Weak rim/fill for characters deep in the hallway only.

Overcast rainy daylight through the big right-wall window (same quality from the whole window row), sampled (91,95,101) -- dim neutral-cool grey-blue. Listed 'cool cyan-white' is directionally right; keep intensity low, it is a soft screen-right fill, not a sun key.

reflective_surfaces.note: Large grey-blue rain puddle on the hallway tile along the right baseboard (leaves floating in it), cut off by the bottom frame edge between x~700-882. Traced visually from a 2x gridded crop; verified against the calibration overlay render.
Small separate wet smear just left of the main puddle.
Smaller wet patch below the middle right-wall window (rain blown in), mid-ground depth.

camera.shot_scale: wide establishing (hallway master; location plate shared by 3 panels)
camera.angle: one-point perspective down the hall, eye-level slightly low (chibi eye-height), locked-off

decompression_note: Was MZ-2026-09-02_P01_PANEL02 in the 24-panel edition; same locked beat, renumbered by the 16-page/96-panel format directive.
